content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 3, 1992

Pete S. Pierson, Esq.
Franchise Tax Board
Legal Division
P.O. Box 1468
Sacramento, CA 95812-1468

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-539a

Dear Mr. Pierson:

You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you on
October 27, 1992, on behalf of the Franchise Tax Board concerning
the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ This letter confirms my advice.

We have previously provided you advice in the Goldberg Advice
Letter, No. A-92-474 and the Pierson Advice Letter, No. A-92-539,
regarding the application of Section 89001 to references to the
Franchise Tax Board and to individual members of the Franchise Tax
Board in various tax booklets.

Based on a review of the materials you provided on
October 27, 1992, which you contemplate will be included in a
1992 tax booklet, I advised you that it is permissible to include
within the various tax booklets published by the Franchise Tax
Board a facsimile of a letter to taxpayers signed by the Executive
Officer of the Franchise Tax Board, which makes references to the
Franchise Tax Board but does not contain the names or any other
reference to individual board members.

I also advised you that Regulation 18901(b)(1) does not
prohibit the inclusion of the names and titles of the members of
the Franchise Tax Board in the front cover of the tax booklets.
However, you may only include the names of the officials and the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-539a
Page 2


titles which identify them as either the chairman or a member of
the Franchise Tax Board.  Moreover, the names and titles must
appear in the same type size, typeface, type color, and location.

     If I can be of any further assistance to you, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.2


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        Luisa Menchaca
                                        Counsel, Legal Division


LM:docs:92539a




2  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.conclusion: It is permissible to include within the various tax booklets published by the Franchise Tax Board a facsimile of a letter to taxpayers signed by the Executive Officer of the Franchise Tax Board, which makes references to the Franchise Tax Board but does not contain the names or any other reference to individual board members. Regulation 18901(b)(1) does not prohibit the inclusion of the names and titles of the members of the Franchise Tax Board in the front cover of the tax booklets, provided the names and titles identify them as either the chairman or a member of the Franchise Tax Board and appear in the same type size, typeface, type color, and location.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Franchise Tax Board may include facsimiles of letters from its Executive Officer and the names/titles of board members in tax booklets under the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the Franchise Tax Board may include a facsimile letter from its Executive Officer in tax booklets and may list board member names and titles on the front cover, so long as references do not include individual board members' names in the letter itself and any cover listing uses consistent formatting and identifies members by their board positions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior oral advice (October 27, 1992). No formal QUESTION section header present, but the substance of the legal question is clearly stated in the first paragraph. The document references prior advice letters (A-92-474 and A-92-539). Confidence reduced slightly due to lack of traditional section headers and the confirmation-of-prior-advice nature of the letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Franchise Tax Board may include facsimiles of letters from its Executive Officer and the names/titles of board members in tax booklets under the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions.

CONCLUSION: It is permissible to include within the various tax booklets published by the Franchise Tax Board a facsimile of a letter to taxpayers signed by the Executive Officer of the Franchise Tax Board, which makes references to the Franchise Tax Board but does not contain the names or any other reference to individual board members. Regulation 18901(b)(1) does not prohibit the inclusion of the names and titles of the members of the Franchise Tax Board in the front cover of the tax booklets, provided the names and titles identify them as either the chairman or a member of the Franchise Tax Board and appear in the same type size, typeface, type color, and location.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming prior oral advice to the Franchise Tax Board regarding permissible inclusion of executive correspondence and board member identification in 1992 tax booklets under the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions.
